title: service-accounts — retention windows
description: SA retention is compliance-driven, not product-driven. Soft-deleted SAs carry a 90-day retention hold before hard delete (SOC 2 audit-evidence window; longer than typical human-actor retention because inter-service audit correlation extends further). JWT audit logs live 365 days hot + 7 years cold. Secret hashes are overwritten on rotation (no history). last_used_at + last_used_ip retained for the SA's lifetime.
compliance_regimes_referenced: soc2 CC6.7 (prompt access removal) — SAs disabled + soft-deleted within the operator's action; hard delete within 90d + retention-hold-days
soc2 CC7.2 (system monitoring) — dormant SA detection weekly; suspicious-activity fingerprint retained rolling 30d
nist SP 800-131A — signer_kid material owned by auth module; rotation logs retained per that module's policy
owasp asvs v6 — credential-lifecycle audit trail retained via activity_log + audit_log tables

hot.days: while is_enabled = true AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now())

purge.trigger: explicit soft-delete via `service-account:delete` OR /platform/service-accounts DELETE endpoint OR auto-expiry via PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob
purge.action: soft-delete (deleted_at + is_enabled=false); keeps secret_hash + metadata for retention hold
purge.notes: Row is NEVER hard-deleted while inbound activity_log or audit rows would lose their reference; hard delete replaces user_id/user_type with `[REDACTED-SERVICE-ACCOUNT]` on referencing audit rows.

purge.trigger: expires_at + grace_days_after_expiry < now() AND is_enabled = true
purge.action: soft-disable (is_enabled=false); fires ServiceAccountExpired; then follows the soft-deleted retention flow

purge.trigger: any secret rotation OR SA hard-delete
purge.action: overwrite in-place on rotation; hard-delete removes the row entirely
purge.notes: There is NO password-history retention. SA secrets are randomly generated + rotated frequently; reuse prevention is not a realistic threat.

During the lifetime of the SA, last_used_at is continuously overwritten with the most recent request timestamp — there is no historical usage log at the row level. Historical usage lives in activity_log rows (with their own retention).

purge.action: move to cold storage (S3 + parquet); retain 7 years total (SOC 2 evidence window)
purge.job: activity module owns the retention pipeline; this module only writes

purge.action: move to cold storage; retain 7 years total
purge.notes: SA-referencing audit rows have their user_id anonymised to `[REDACTED-SERVICE-ACCOUNT]` when the SA is hard-deleted; audit content is preserved for compliance evidence.

purge.trigger: tuple observation older than 30 days OR cache eviction
purge.action: trim from the SortedSet on next insert
purge.notes: Rolling window — the SortedSet's score is the observation timestamp, so trimming to the last 30 days is O(log N).

hot.days: matches the JWT's remaining exp — at most jwt.max_ttl_seconds

purge.trigger: TTL expiration on the cache key
purge.action: cache-native TTL cleanup; no scheduled job needed
purge.notes: The deny-list entry outlives the JWT's exp by AT MOST clock-skew tolerance; after that, the JWT would be rejected on exp check regardless.

hold_period_extensions.description: The retention hold can be extended (never shortened below the default) by compliance officers when a legal-hold applies to the SA's audit trail. Extension is stored as a per-SA metadata flag (`retention_hold_extended_until`) that overrides the config default. PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob honours the per-SA override.
hold_period_extensions.how_to_extend: Via `service-account:describe {id} --set-hold-until=<date>` (proposed CLI extension, deferred to Wave 1b)
hold_period_extensions.audit_trail: Every extension is written to the compliance-audit log with the compliance officer as actor + free-form justification.
